title: ptarmica olympica
intro: Ptarmica olympica, also known as European elecampane, is a perennial plant belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly the roots, which contain inulin and sesquiterpene lactones, including alantolactone. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making the plant beneficial for treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Ptarmica olympica include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
